The **2009–10 La Liga** (known as the *Liga BBVA* for sponsorship reasons) was the 79th season of [La Liga](/wiki/La_Liga "La Liga") since its establishment. [Barcelona](/wiki/FC_Barcelona "FC Barcelona") were the defending champions, having won their 19th La Liga title in [the previous season](/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_La_Liga "2008–09 La Liga"). The campaign began on 29 August 2009, and concluded on 16 May 2010; all top-flight European leagues ended earlier than the previous season due to the impending [2010 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2010_FIFA_World_Cup "2010 FIFA World Cup").
A total of 20 teams contested the league, 17 of which already contested in the 2008–09 season, and three of which were promoted from the [Segunda División](/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n "2008–09 Segunda División"). In addition, a new match ball – the [Nike](/wiki/Nike,_Inc. "Nike, Inc.") T90 Ascente – served as the official ball for all matches.

On 16 May 2010, Barcelona secured a second consecutive La Liga title, and twentieth league title overall, following a 4–0 home victory against [Valladolid](/wiki/Real_Valladolid "Real Valladolid"). Barcelona's [Lionel Messi](/wiki/Lionel_Messi "Lionel Messi") won the [La Liga award](/wiki/La_Liga_Awards "La Liga Awards") for Best Player for a second straight season.

[Real Madrid](/wiki/Real_Madrid_CF "Real Madrid CF")'s 96 points made them the runners-up with the highest points total in the history of [Europe's top five leagues](/wiki/Big_Five_(association_football) "Big Five (association football)"), until this was surpassed by [Liverpool](/wiki/Liverpool_F.C. "Liverpool F.C.")'s 97 points in the [2018–19 Premier League](/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_Premier_League "2018–19 Premier League").[[4]](#cite_note-Bleacher_Report-4)
